target.title: fix(gateway): register SIGHUP handler for daemon signal hygiene
target.body:
## Summary

Register a supervisor-gated SIGHUP handler in the gateway run-loop as standard daemon hygiene.

When the gateway detects it is running under a respawn supervisor (launchd, systemd, or schtasks), SIGHUP is caught, logged, and ignored. Foreground (unsupervised) gateways retain Node.js default behavior (exit 129).

This prevents accidental gateway termination when a terminal hangup signal leaks through to a supervised daemon that has no controlling terminal.

> **Note:** This is defensive hardening. The SSH disconnect outage reported in #84249 involves a SIGTERM path from the TUI/systemd lifecycle chain, which is a separate concern. This PR ensures the gateway does not crash on any stray SIGHUP regardless of how it was launched.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `run-loop.ts` | Add supervisor-gated SIGHUP handler: detect supervisor via `eagerLifecycleRuntime.detectRespawnSupervisor()`, register handler only when supervised |
| `run-loop.test.ts` | Two SIGHUP tests + supervisor mock updates for existing tests |

## Behavior

| Mode | SIGHUP behavior | Why |
|------|----------------|-----|
| Supervised (launchd/systemd/schtasks) | Logged + ignored | Daemon has no controlling terminal; SIGHUP is accidental |
| Foreground (no supervisor) | Node.js default (exit 129) | No behavior change from current main |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.test.ts
 ✓ cli src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.test.ts (37 tests) 181ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  37 passed (37)
```

SIGHUP-specific tests:
```
 ✓ runGatewayLoop > ignores SIGHUP when running under a supervisor (3ms)
 ✓ runGatewayLoop > does not install SIGHUP handler in foreground (no supervisor) (2ms)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Gateway process terminates with exit 129 on SIGHUP because no signal handler is registered. Any supervised daemon that receives a stray SIGHUP (e.g. from a parent terminal closing) would crash.

- **Real environment tested**: Docker `node:22-bookworm` (Node.js `v22.22.3`, Debian bookworm), OpenClaw checkout at PR head `ec8faa55`.

- **Failing proof before fix**: On current main branch (`0185d0d2`), no SIGHUP handler exists:

```
$ grep -n 'SIGHUP' src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts
(no matches)

$ grep -n 'process.on.*SIG' src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.ts
782:  process.on("SIGTERM", onSigterm);
783:  process.on("SIGINT", onSigint);
784:  process.on("SIGUSR1", onSigusr1);
```

Live signal test on main — process killed by SIGHUP:
```
$ node test-no-handler.mjs &
PID: 260
SIGHUP listeners: 0
Waiting for signals...

$ kill -HUP 260
bash: line 15:   260 Hangup                  node test-no-handler.mjs
Process 260 terminated after SIGHUP with exit code: 129
```

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```bash
git checkout pr-84422
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/gateway-cli/run-loop.test.ts
```

Plus live signal tests simulating both supervisor-gated paths from the PR code.

- **Evidence after fix**:

Supervised mode (SIGHUP handler registered, simulates `detectRespawnSupervisor()` returning `"launchd"`):
```
$ node test-supervised.mjs &
PID: 464
SIGHUP listeners: 1
Supervisor: launchd
Waiting for signals...

--- Sending first SIGHUP ---
signal SIGHUP received; ignoring (launchd supervised daemon survives terminal hangup)
Process 464 still alive after first SIGHUP ✓

--- Sending second SIGHUP ---
signal SIGHUP received; ignoring (launchd supervised daemon survives terminal hangup)
Process 464 still alive after second SIGHUP ✓

--- Sending SIGTERM (clean shutdown) ---
SIGTERM received, shutting down gracefully
Process exited with code: 0 ✓
```

Foreground mode (no supervisor detected, no SIGHUP handler — Node.js default preserved):
```
$ node test-foreground.mjs &
PID: 482
SIGHUP listeners: 0
Supervisor: null
Waiting for signals (foreground mode, no SIGHUP handler)...

--- Sending SIGHUP to foreground process ---
bash: line 25:   482 Hangup                  node test-foreground.mjs
Process 482 terminated with exit code: 129 (Node.js default SIGHUP) ✓
```

- **Observed result after fix**: Under supervision, SIGHUP is logged and ignored — process survives multiple SIGHUP signals and only stops on SIGTERM (exit 0). Without supervision (foreground), no SIGHUP handler is registered and Node.js default behavior is preserved (exit 129). This matches the PR's supervisor-gated design.

- **What was not tested**: Live production gateway with actual launchd/systemd supervision receiving SIGHUP during SSH disconnect. The signal behavior is validated at the process level; the supervisor detection logic (`detectRespawnSupervisor`) is tested separately in its own test suite.

## Compatibility

No `kill -HUP` workflow exists in the OpenClaw codebase or documentation:
```
$ grep -rn 'kill.*HUP\|kill -1' docs/ scripts/ src/ | grep -v node_modules | grep -v .test.
(zero hits)
```

Documented signal contract (`docs/cli/gateway.md`): SIGUSR1=restart, SIGINT/SIGTERM=stop. SIGHUP was previously undefined (Node.js default). This PR only affects supervised daemons; foreground behavior is unchanged.
target.author: jerry-xin
